channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel halts entry of aid to Gaza until Hamas agrees to new ceasefire deal
item.description: Israel has halted the entry of aid into Gaza until Hamas agrees to a new ceasefire deal, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israel blocks humanitarian aid into Gaza after Hamas rejects ceasefire extension proposal
item.description: Israel has cut all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip and warned Hamas it would face "additional consequences" if it does not accept a new deal for an extended ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's Starmer says Europe is at crossroads in history as world leaders discuss ending war in Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer says Britain will use 1.6 billion pounds ($2 billion) in export financing to supply 5,000 air defense missiles for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Uruguay's new leftist president takes office, facing a financial balancing act
item.description: Yamandú Orsi, a telegenic left-leaning former mayor and history teacher, has taken office as Uruguay’s new president.
item.dc:creator: Matilde Campodonico And Isabel Debre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's military is told to prepare to defend a Druze community outside Syria's capital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense ministry on Saturday said the military has been instructed to prepare to defend a Druze settlement in the suburbs of Damascus, asserting that the minority it has vowed to protect was “under attack” by Syrian forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy embraced by British prime minister a day after White House blowout
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy he has the nation’s unwavering support a day after the blowout at the White House with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kurdish PKK militants declare a ceasefire in 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey declared a ceasefire on Saturday in what could mark a significant boost to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government, two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Abdulrahman Shaheen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Namibian founding president Sam Nujoma is laid to rest and praised as the last African liberator
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia’s founding president, Sam Nujoma, who steered the country to freedom from apartheid South Africa, was laid to rest in the country’s Heroes Acre cemetery on Saturday following a state funeral attended by African leaders and foreign dignitaries.
item.dc:creator: SONJA SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelenskyy-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Getting the Israel-Hamas ceasefire to the next phase will be difficult. Here's why
item.description: Israel and Hamas have begun working to advance their ceasefire agreement in Gaza to the next phase, but it's unclear if they'll get there and, if not, what comes next.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's the real reason Trump and Zelenskyy's deal blew up in the Oval Office
item.description: A meeting between Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and President Donald Trump exploded after Zelenskyy rejected a deal that did not include security guarantees for his country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladeshi students who led uprising that ousted ex-premier Sheikh Hasina form new political party
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Students in Bangladesh who led a mass uprising to topple former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina last summer are now diving into politics by forming the new National Citizen Party. The aim is to create new political space in a fiercely divisive dynastic political landscape.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Myanmar online news site says its jailed journalist is tortured for revealing abuses of prisoners
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — An independent online news agency in Myanmar said Friday that one of its journalists arrested two years ago has been subjected to daily physical and mental abuse after exposing human rights violations in the country’s main prison where he's being held.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suicide bomb at a seminary in northwest Pakistan kills top cleric and 5 others ahead of Ramadan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — An attacker blew himself up Friday in a mosque at a pro-Taliban seminary in northwestern Pakistan, killing a top cleric and five other worshippers and wounding dozens ahead of the fasting month of Ramadan, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippines shark attack: 2 tourists killed at popular diving spot
item.description: Two tourists reportedly were killed by sharks in the Philippines near Verde Island after getting swept away by a current while diving at a popular location
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Thailand warns Americans of 'violent retaliatory attacks' risk after Uyghurs deported to China
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bangkok is warning Americans of potential "violent retaliatory attacks" after a group of Uyghurs were deported by Thailand to China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A good rapport but mixed signals on Ukraine: Takeaways from Keir Starmer's trip to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prime Minister Keir Starmer wore a broad smile aboard the plane home from Washington. He landed back in Britain on Friday with the satisfaction of a tricky mission not quite accomplished, but off to a flying start.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin 'doesn't want peace,' EU chief diplomat warns ahead of Trump, Zelenskyy deal
item.description: The EU's chief diplomat has warned President Trump that Russian President Putin "doesn't really want peace" as Trump heads into a deal with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: More 'city-killer' asteroids apparent, but fret not: astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation
